subject: Conditional Probability and Independence
question: The probability of a applicant to be admitted to a certain college is 0.8. The probability for a student in the college to live on campus is 0.6. What is the probability that an applicant will be admitted to the college and will be assigned a dormitory housing?
explanation: The probability of the applicant being admitted and receiving dormitory housing is defined by $\Pr(\text{Accepted and Housing}) = \Pr(\text{Housing} \mid \text{Accepted})\Pr(\text{Accepted}) = (0.6)(0.8) = 0.48$ Equation (9.1) can be generalized to any finite number of events. Theorem 9.1 Consider $n$ events $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_n$. Then $\Pr(A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n) = \Pr(A_1)\Pr(A_2 \mid A_1)\Pr(A_3 \mid A_1 \cap A_2) \ldots \Pr(A_n \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_{n-1})$ Proof. The proof is by induction on $n \ge 2$. By Equation (9.1), the relation holds for $n = 2$. Suppose that the relation is true for $2, 3, \ldots, n$. We wish to establish $\Pr(A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_{n+1}) = \Pr(A_1)\Pr(A_2 \mid A_1)\Pr(A_3 \mid A_1 \cap A_2) \ldots \Pr(A_{n+1} \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n)$ We have $\Pr(A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_{n+1}) = \Pr((A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n) \cap A_{n+1}) = \Pr(A_{n+1} \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n)\Pr(A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n) = \Pr(A_{n+1} \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n)\Pr(A_1)\Pr(A_2 \mid A_1)\Pr(A_3 \mid A_1 \cap A_2) \ldots \Pr(A_n \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_{n-1}) = \Pr(A_1)\Pr(A_2 \mid A_1)\Pr(A_3 \mid A_1 \cap A_2) \ldots \Pr(A_n \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_{n-1}) \times \Pr(A_{n+1} \mid A_1 \cap A_2 \cap \ldots \cap A_n)$
